structured_payload_candidate.title: Plant Disable Actions
structured_payload_candidate.equipment_scope: Boiler plant
structured_payload_candidate.summary: When the plant is disabled: (a) Shut off the enabled boiler(s). For headered primary pumps: (b) close HW isolation valve(s) after 3 minutes and disable operating HW pump(s) per Section 5.21.6. For dedicated primary pumps: (c) disable operating primary HW pump(s) per Section 5.21.6. For primary-secondary plants: (d) disable operating secondary HW pump(s) per Section 5.21.7.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Stage Availability Definition
structured_payload_candidate.equipment_scope: Boiler plant
structured_payload_candidate.summary: A stage shall be deemed unavailable if the stage cannot be achieved because a boiler required to operate in the stage is faulted per Section 5.1.15.5.b.1.iii or a hot water pump dedicated to that boiler is faulted per Section 5.1.15.5.b.1.i; otherwise the stage shall be deemed available.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Staging Execution Requirements
structured_payload_candidate.equipment_scope: Boiler plant
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Staging shall be executed subject to: (a) each stage shall have a minimum runtime of 10 minutes; (b) timers shall reset to zero at the completion of every stage change; (c) any unavailable stage shall be skipped during staging events, but staging conditionals in the current stage shall be evaluated as per usual.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Enforce 10-min minimum runtime per stage; reset timers on stage change; skip unavailable stages.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote:

a. Each stage shall have a minimum runtime of 10 minutes.
b. Timers shall reset to zero at the completion of every stage change.
c. Any unavailable stage (see Section 5.21.3.8 ) shall be skipped during staging events, but staging conditionals in the current stage shall be evaluated as per usual.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Hybrid Plant Staging Exceptions
structured_payload_candidate.equipment_scope: Hybrid boiler plants
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Exceptions for hybrid plants: (i) If the highest condensing boiler stage is unavailable, the stage up conditionals in the next lower condensing boiler stage shall be those from the highest condensing boiler stage. (ii) If the lowest non-condensing boiler stage is unavailable, the stage down conditionals of the next higher non-condensing boiler stage shall be those from the lowest non-condensing boiler stage.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Primary-Only Condensing Boiler Stage Up Conditions
structured_payload_candidate.equipment_scope: Primary-only condensing boiler plants
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Stage up if any of the following is true: (1) Availability Condition: The equipment necessary to operate the current stage are unavailable (not subject to minimum stage runtime). (2) Efficiency Condition: Both Qrequired exceeds 200% of B-STAGE MIN of the next available stage for 10 minutes and hot water flowrate exceeds the minimum flow setpoint of the next available stage. (3) Failsafe Condition: HW supply temperature is 10°F < setpoint for 15 minutes.
structured_payload_candidate.trigger_condition: Availability, efficiency, or failsafe condition met
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Stage up.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote:
Stage up if any of the following is true:
1. Availability Condition: The equipment necessary to operate the current stage are unavailable. The availability condition is not subject to the minimum stage runtime requirement. Or
2. Efficiency Condition: Both of the following are true:
i. Qrequired  exceeds 200% of B-STAGE MIN of the next available stage for 10 minutes
ii. Hot water flowrate exceeds the minimum flow setpoint of the next available stage (see Section 5.21.8 ).
3. Failsafe Condition: HW supply temperature is 1 0°F < setpoint for 15 minutes.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Lag Boiler Enable Sequence with Minimum Flow Bypass Valve and Pony Boiler
structured_payload_candidate.equipment_scope: Boiler plant with minimum flow bypass valve and pony boiler
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Whenever a lag boiler is enabled: (a) For any stage change during which a smaller boiler is disabled and a larger boiler is enabled, slowly change the minimum flow bypass setpoint to that appropriate for the stage transition as indicated in Section 5.21.8.2. After new setpoint is achieved, wait 1 minute to allow loop to stabilize. (b) For any other stage change, reset the minimum flow bypass setpoint to that appropriate for the new stage as indicated in Section 5.21.8.1. (c) Start the next lag boiler's primary pump (dedicated pumps). (d) Open the next lag boiler's isolation valve (primary-only headered variable speed). (e) Start the next lag primary pump and simultaneously open the next lag boiler's isolation valve (other headered). (f) After 30 seconds, enable the lag boiler. (g) For any stage change during which a smaller boiler is disabled and a larger boiler is enabled: (1) Wait 5 minutes for the newly enabled boiler to prove that it is operating correctly (not faulted as defined in Section 5.1.15.5.b.1.iii), then shut off the smaller boiler. (2) After 3 minutes, turn off the smaller boiler's primary pump (dedicated). (3) After 3 minutes, close the smaller boiler's isolation valve (primary-only headered variable speed). (4) After 3 minutes, turn off the last lag primary pump and simultaneously close the smaller boiler's isolation valve (other headered). (5) Change the minimum flow bypass setpoint to that appropriate for the new stage as indicated in Section 5.21.8.1.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Boiler Load Calculation
structured_payload_candidate.equipment_scope: All plants other than those with variable flow primary loops without flow meters
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Calculate load for each operating boiler using flowrate through the boiler, FLOW B; hot water return temperature entering the boiler, HWRT B; and hot water supply temperature leaving the boiler, HWST B. QD = 0.49 * FLOW B * (HWST B - HWRT B) [kbtu/h]. Inputs determined per rules: (a) If flow not individually measured but primary circuit flow measured, FLOW B assumed proportional to design flow through all operating boilers. (b) If constant primary loop and no flow measured, FLOW B assumed equal to design flowrate for current stage. (c) HWRT B from hardwired sensor if available, else internal boiler sensor via network; if multiple boilers, average of operating boilers. (d) HWST B from hardwired sensor if available, else internal boiler sensor via network; only if neither available, use common supply temperature sensor.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Compute QD per boiler; use available sensors and flow assumptions as described.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote:
Boiler Load. Calculate load for each operating boiler (as applicable) using flowra te through the boiler, FLOW B; hot water return temperature entering the boiler, HWRT B; and hot water supply temperature leaving the boiler, HWST B. Inputs to the below equation shall be determined per the following rules.
𝑄𝐷= 0.49∗𝐹𝐿𝑂 𝑊𝐵(𝐻𝑊𝑆 𝑇𝐵−𝐻𝑊𝑅𝑇𝐵)[𝑘𝑏𝑡𝑢
ℎ]
Where flow through each boiler is individually measured using a flow meter, FLOW B shall be the flow measured by the boiler’s associated flow meter.
Retain the following section  for plants with parallel boilers where flowrate through e ach boiler is not measured, but flowrate through the primary circuit is measured. Delete otherwise.
a. FLOW B shall be assumed proportional to design flow through all operating boilers in the circuit.
Retain the following section  for plants with constant prima ry loop where neither flowrate through the boiler nor flowrate through the primary loop is measured. Delete otherwise.
b. FLOW B shall be assumed equal to the design flowrate through the boiler for the current stage as determined during balancing.
c. HWRT B shall be the return temperature entering the boiler as read by a hardwired BAS sensor if available. If a hardwired sensor is unavailable, temperature shall be read from a sensor internal to the boiler through its network interface. If multiple boilers are enable d, the temperature shall be the average return temperature read from the operating boilers through the network interface.
d. HWST B shall be a hardwired temperature sensor at the outlet of the equipment if available. If a hardwired sensor is unavailable, tempe rature shall be read from a sensor internal to the boiler through its network interface. Only if neither of the above is available shall a common supply temperature sensor (i.e. one measuring the output from multiple boilers), be used.
